examples.motionText: Children should be allowed to decide their own bedtime.
examples.flawedArgument: If children choose bedtime, soon no child will ever sleep properly again.

examples.motionText: Children should be allowed to decide their own bedtime.
examples.flawedArgument: Either parents control bedtime or children become unhealthy.

examples.motionText: Children should be allowed to decide their own bedtime.
examples.flawedArgument: Kids always know the best bedtime for themselves.

examples.motionText: Parents should limit their kids' screen time.
examples.flawedArgument: Children use screens more, so screens caused all behavior problems.

examples.motionText: Parents should limit their kids' screen time.
examples.flawedArgument: Screens completely destroy children's brains.

examples.motionText: Parents should limit their kids' screen time.
examples.flawedArgument: My cousin got addicted to games, so all kids will.

examples.motionText: Year-round schooling is better than the traditional school calendar.
examples.flawedArgument: One school improved grades, so year-round schooling is always better.

examples.motionText: Year-round schooling is better than the traditional school calendar.
examples.flawedArgument: Year-round schooling changes the schedule, so students become more successful.

examples.motionText: Year-round schooling is better than the traditional school calendar.
examples.flawedArgument: Either schools switch to year-round schooling or education will fail.

examples.motionText: Classrooms should have no more than 20 students.
examples.flawedArgument: One small class had good grades, so small classes automatically improve learning.

examples.motionText: Classrooms should have no more than 20 students.
examples.flawedArgument: Large classrooms never work.

examples.motionText: Classrooms should have no more than 20 students.
examples.flawedArgument: Smaller classes mean better education without explaining why.

examples.motionText: Kids should learn how to grow their own food.
examples.flawedArgument: Gardening teaches responsibility, so children will become better adults.

examples.motionText: Kids should learn how to grow their own food.
examples.flawedArgument: Every child needs farming skills to survive.

examples.motionText: Primary school students should be encouraged to read English comics.
examples.flawedArgument: One student improved English through comics, so comics work for everyone.

examples.motionText: Primary school students should be encouraged to read English comics.
examples.flawedArgument: You just want children to stop reading real books.

examples.motionText: Primary school students should learn English grammar before vocabulary.
examples.flawedArgument: Students must learn grammar first or they can never learn English.

examples.motionText: Primary school students should learn English grammar before vocabulary.
examples.flawedArgument: Vocabulary is useless without grammar.

examples.motionText: AI can be trusted to control self-driving cars.
examples.flawedArgument: AI cars never make mistakes.

examples.motionText: Short video apps should be banned.
examples.flawedArgument: If we allow short videos, children will stop studying forever.

examples.motionText: Students should only study for half the day and spend the other half outdoors.
examples.flawedArgument: More outdoor time means all students will become healthier and smarter.

examples.motionText: School cafeterias should let students choose the menu.
examples.flawedArgument: Either students choose the menu or schools ignore student health.

examples.motionText: AI astronauts are better suited for dangerous missions than humans.
examples.flawedArgument: Humans are completely useless in dangerous space missions.

examples.motionText: AI and programming courses are more important than traditional labor courses.
examples.flawedArgument: You want schools to get rid of all art and sports classes.

examples.motionText: It's better to read one book deeply than many books quickly.
examples.flawedArgument: I learned a lot from one book, so deep reading is always better.

examples.motionText: Electric vehicles should replace gasoline cars by 2030.
examples.flawedArgument: If we do not switch now, the planet will become unlivable immediately.

examples.motionText: Education relies on testing too much.
examples.flawedArgument: Schools only care about test scores.

examples.motionText: Zoos should be banned.
examples.flawedArgument: Either we ban zoos or animals will always suffer.
